questions.question: What does your analytics stack look like today — what tools are your data scientists using day-to-day?
questions.intent: Maps the competitive landscape. Identifies what to position against.
questions.followUp: Are they happy with those tools, or are there pain points?

questions.question: How many people on your team actually build models vs. consume insights from them?
questions.intent: Sizing question. Also reveals if business users need self-service tools.
questions.followUp: Do business users have any way to run analyses without the data team?

questions.question: What's the biggest bottleneck in getting a model from development to production?
questions.intent: Universal pain point. Opens MLOps conversation regardless of industry.
questions.followUp: How long does that typically take — weeks or months?

questions.question: Are you running on-premises, cloud, or hybrid — and is there a cloud migration strategy in play?
questions.intent: Deployment architecture question. SAS Viya runs anywhere — this is a strength.
questions.followUp: Which cloud provider are you standardizing on?

questions.question: How do you govern your models — is there a review process, performance monitoring, or bias detection?
questions.intent: Model governance is often missing. Opens responsible AI conversation.
questions.followUp: Who is accountable when a model underperforms or causes harm?

questions.question: What's driving this analytics investment right now — a specific business problem, a regulation, or a strategic initiative?
questions.intent: Identifies the buying trigger. Helps position SAS to the real need.
questions.followUp: Is there a timeline or event driving urgency?

questions.question: Do you have a data quality or data management practice — or are teams mostly managing their own data?
questions.intent: Bad data = bad models. Opens data management conversation.
questions.followUp: How much time does your team spend on data prep vs. actual modeling?

questions.question: If you had to explain to your board how a model-driven decision was made, could you do that today?
questions.intent: Explainability test. Particularly relevant for regulated industries but universal concern.
questions.followUp: Is that a current board-level concern or something on the horizon?

questions.question: What does success look like for this project — is there a specific KPI or outcome you're targeting?
questions.intent: Grounds the conversation in measurable outcomes. Helps build ROI story.
questions.followUp: How will you measure whether the investment paid off?

questions.question: Who else is involved in this decision — procurement, IT security, the business unit requesting it?
questions.intent: Maps the buying committee. Essential for deal strategy.
questions.followUp: Would it be helpful to bring a more technical session for the IT team separately?
